steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what motivates Tía Ana's repeated checking. Look for the detail in the passage that directly explains why she keeps coming in to see if guests have left.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — She is frustrated because she needs assistance elsewhere in the house, but Salomé is unavailable while entertaining the guests.: the passage never mentions Tía Ana needing help from Salomé or being needed elsewhere in the house. ✗
  • Choice B — She is anxious for the gathering to disperse so that she can ready the space for her own needs.: the passage states the parlor is her special province for her school, and that after every salon it is “never in order for its transformation back to a classroom the following morning.” Her repeated checking is explained directly by this. ✓
  • Choice C — She is impatient to share her plans to start a new school with the guests and hopes they will support her.: the passage says nothing about Tía Ana wanting to talk to the guests—she wants them gone, not engaged. ✗
  • Choice D — She finds the guests to be dull and is eager to find a way to leave the salon.: her motivation is about reclaiming her space, not her opinion of the guests' personalities. ✗
B is correct.
